Percentage increase is a useful thing to calculate when comparing time periods, estimating growth percent (yearly, monthly, daily etc.), or comparing a new state to an old state of things, e.g. a new [salary](https://www.gigacalculator.com/converters/salary-converter.php) or [hourly rate](https://www.gigacalculator.com/calculators/hourly-to-salary-calculator.php), versus your previous one. In this article, we will explore what YoY analysis is, how it is calculated, and why it is useful. We will also look at the advantages and disadvantages of YoY analysis and how to calculate YoY growth in Excel. Whether you are a financial analyst, investor, or business owner, understanding YoY analysis can help you spot trends and make informed decisions. ## What is Year Over Year (YOY)? YoY stands for Year over Year and is a type of financial analysis used to compare results from a period of time in one year to the same period of time in the prior year. YoY analysis is widely used in finance and [economic analysis](https://www.federalreservehistory.org/essays/great-inflation) and is helpful for quickly understanding growth trends from year to year. ## YoY Formula The YoY formula is defined as the current year\u2019s value divided by the previous year\u2019s value minus 1: ![Image 1: YoY](https://propertymetrics.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/12/image-57.png) For example, suppose [net operating income](https://propertymetrics.com/blog/net-operating-income/) for a property was 120,000 last year. And the year previous year net operating income was 105,000. In this case, the YoY growth was 120,000 / 105,000 -1, or 14.3% An alternative YoY formula takes the current value minus the previous value and then divides that result by the previous value: ![Image 2: YoY Formula](https://propertymetrics.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/12/image-56.png) Using the same example, we could calculate the YoY growth by taking our 120,000 net operating income and subtracting the previous year\u2019s NOI of 105,000, then dividing the result by 105,000. In this case, that would be (120,000 \u2013 105,000) / 105,000, which gives us the same year-over-year growth of 14.3%. The YoY formula can also be used to calculate the dollar amount of growth or the current or previous values. For example, if the average rent in a particular geographic area is currently 1,100 and YoY growth was reported as 10%, then last year the average rent was 1,100 / (1 + 10%), or 1,000.\n...\nTitle: Year Over Year (YOY) Analysis: A Simple Guide URL Source: https://propertymetrics.com/blog/year-over-year-yoy/ Published Time: 2022-02-14T20:37:26+00:00 Markdown Content: Year over Year (YoY) analysis is a powerful tool for financial analysis that provides a direct comparison of performance between two periods of time. In this article, we will explore what YoY analysis is, how it is calculated, and why it is useful. We will also look at the advantages and disadvantages of YoY analysis and how to calculate YoY growth in Excel. Whether you are a financial analyst, investor, or business owner, understanding YoY a", "relevance_score": 0.8}, {"title": "How I Built a BetterTeamandGrewRevenueto... - Indie Hackers", "url": "https://www.indiehackers.com/post/how-i-built-a-better-team-and-grew-revenue-to-50k-mrr-OoinI1NQqGuMXMMUm7hc", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] Today, oneyearafter launching, Systeme.io is at 1,500 paying customers and $50K in monthly recurringrevenue.Ourcompetitorshaverevenuesbetween $10M and $100Mperyear, so I believe that, even if we do a bad job, we will land around $5Mperyearinrevenue.", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "YoY (Year-over-Year): Definition, Formula, and Examples", "url": "https://www.careerprinciples.com/resources/yoy-year-over-year-definition-formula-and-examples", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] YoYYearoverYearFormula.
